title: Sizzling Russian Rice Onigiri
description: Sizzling Russian Rice Onigiri delivers a fresh, molten rice experience in a one‑pot dish. The sweet rice syrup and tangy jocheong give it a unique flavor while vegetarian and quick to prepare.
instructions: Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear, then drain.
In a medium saucepan, combine rinsed rice, water, rice syrup, soy sauce, ginger, and garlic; bring to a gentle boil over medium heat.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Remove the lid and stir in scallions, dill, pepper, and butter until melted and the mixture is glossy.
Transfer the hot rice mixture to a heat‑proof bowl and let it sit for 2 minutes to cool slightly before shaping.
Wet your hands with a little water, then grab a tablespoon of rice, flatten it, place a small square of sweet jocheong in the center, and seal the rice around it into a neat ball.
Arrange the shaped onigiri on a serving plate and drizzle a thin line of additional rice syrup over the top.
Heat a nonstick skillet over medium‑high heat and sear the onigiri from all sides until golden brown and sizzling, about 2–3 minutes per side.
Serve immediately while the onigiri remains molten inside, garnished with extra dill if desired.
